universe.name: Ylem
universe.description: A world of golden coasts, whispering forests, and ancient secrets buried beneath stone and sea. The kingdom of Beausoleil clings to the western shore, its cities bright against the endless blue.

inline.name: Le Plan Matériel
inline.description: The prime material plane — where mortal feet tread on solid earth, and the sun rises each dawn over the golden coast.

inline.name: Valterre
inline.description: A vast continent stretching from frozen northern peaks to sun-drenched southern shores. Its western coast, La Côte Dorée, is famed for its mild climate and rich trade.

inline.name: La Côte Dorée
inline.description: The Golden Coast — a ribbon of warm, fertile land between the mountains and the sea. Vineyards climb the hills, fishing boats dot the harbors, and the scent of lavender carries on the breeze.

inline.name: Le Royaume de Beausoleil
inline.description: A proud coastal kingdom ruled by an aging dynasty. Its capital, Plassans, is a city of white stone and terracotta roofs, built where the Auriel River meets the sea.

history.event: The first settlers arrive on the golden coast, drawn by the mild climate and bountiful seas.
Foundation of Plassans as a fishing village at the mouth of the Auriel River.
The Beausoleil dynasty rises to power, unifying the coastal settlements under one banner.
Construction of the Cathédrale Saint-Auriel begins, a monument to the patron saint of the coast.
Montclair is established as a hill fortress to guard against raids from the eastern mountains.
The Great Storm devastates the harbour. Plassans rebuilds stronger, with stone walls and deeper docks.
Present day. Plassans thrives as the jewel of La Côte Dorée, a city of merchants, artisans, and dreamers.

npcs.name: Aurélie Bonnard
npcs.description: A warm, broad-shouldered woman with flour-dusted hands and a ready laugh. She runs La Couronne d'Or with an iron will and a generous heart.

dialogue.greeting: Bienvenue, traveller! A room, a meal, or just a place by the fire?
dialogue.info: Plassans has been peaceful lately, though the fishermen speak of strange lights on the water at night.
dialogue.farewell: May the road rise to meet you, friend.

npcs.name: Père Matthieu
npcs.description: A gaunt elderly man with kind eyes and ink-stained fingers. He tends the Cathédrale with quiet devotion and keeps the city's oldest records.

dialogue.greeting: Peace be upon you, child. The light of Saint-Auriel watches over all who enter here.
dialogue.info: The cathedral has stood for three centuries. Its stones remember much that mortal minds forget.

npcs.name: Madame Lafleur
npcs.description: A sharp-eyed woman in her fifties who knows the value of everything and the price of nothing. Her shop has served Plassans for twenty years.

dialogue.greeting: Ah, a customer! Come in, come in. I have exactly what you need — even if you don't know it yet.
dialogue.info: Trade has been good since the new harbour master arrived. Ships from the east bring rare goods.
dialogue.farewell: Come back soon — I'll save the good stock for you.

npcs.name: Maître Brun
npcs.description: A stocky dwarf with arms like oak beams and a bristling red beard. His forge burns day and night, and his blades are prized across La Côte Dorée.

dialogue.greeting: Aye? Need steel? You've come to the right forge.
dialogue.info: I've been working a new alloy — volcanic ore from the mountains mixed with coastal iron. Stronger than anything I've made before.
dialogue.farewell: Keep your blade sharp and your shield high.

npcs.name: Capitaine Duval
npcs.description: A tall, stern woman with close-cropped grey hair and a scar across her left cheek. She commands the city guard with precision and fairness.

dialogue.greeting: State your business, stranger. Plassans is a peaceful city and we intend to keep it so.
dialogue.info: Bandits have been spotted on the eastern road. We've doubled patrols, but travellers should exercise caution.

npcs.name: Sylvie Herault
npcs.description: A willowy half-elf with silver-streaked auburn hair and eyes that seem to see through you. Her potions are renowned, her prices less so.

dialogue.greeting: The herbs whisper your ailments to me before you speak them. What remedy do you seek?
dialogue.info: Strange fungus has been growing in the sewers beneath the old quarter. I've been studying samples — fascinating, if a touch concerning.

npcs.name: Vieux Jacques
npcs.description: A leathery old sailor turned tavern keeper, missing two fingers and most of his hair. His stories are half lies and entirely entertaining.

dialogue.greeting: Pull up a chair, ya landlubber. Ale's cheap and the stories are free.
dialogue.info: I sailed these waters forty years. There's things beneath the waves that no chart will ever show you.
dialogue.farewell: Don't fall in the harbour on your way out, eh?

npcs.name: Harbour Master Renaud
npcs.description: A portly man with an impressive mustache and an even more impressive collection of maritime charts. He controls all shipping in and out of Plassans.

dialogue.greeting: Yes, yes, the harbour fees are non-negotiable. What else do you need?
dialogue.info: A merchant galley from the eastern isles is expected within the week. They carry silks and spices — and rumours of trouble.

npcs.name: Marcel Dupont
npcs.description: A cheerful fishmonger with a booming voice and a permanent smell of brine. His catch is always the freshest in the market.

dialogue.greeting: Fresh fish! Caught this morning! The finest in all Plassans!
dialogue.info: The fish have been swimming deeper lately. Something's stirring them up out there.
dialogue.farewell: Try the mackerel — you won't regret it!

npcs.name: Guard Thierry
npcs.description: A young guard with eager eyes and polished armor. He takes his duty at the north gate very seriously.

dialogue.greeting: Halt! ... Oh, a traveller. Welcome to Plassans. The Capitaine says I should be more welcoming.
dialogue.info: I heard wolves howling from the northern woods last night. Unusual for this time of year.

npcs.name: Guard Élodie
npcs.description: A guard stationed near the east gate, she keeps a watchful eye on the road from Montclair.

dialogue.greeting: Traveller from the east? The road is clear, but keep your wits about you after dark.
dialogue.info: Merchant caravans have reported missing supplies. Could be bandits, could be worse.

npcs.name: Marie-Claire Fontaine
npcs.description: A baker's wife with rosy cheeks and a warm disposition. She knows every piece of gossip in the city.

dialogue.greeting: Oh, hello dear! Have you tried the brioche from my husband's bakery? Best in the whole coast!
dialogue.info: They say the old Beaumont estate on the hill is haunted. Lights in the windows at night, but no one lives there...
dialogue.farewell: Come by the bakery! Corner of Rue Haute and Rue de l'Ouest!

npcs.name: Henri Beaumont
npcs.description: A thin, pale aristocrat with impeccable dress and a perpetually worried expression. Last of the Beaumont line, he haunts the town hall more than his own manor.

dialogue.greeting: Ah, another adventurer. How... quaint. I don't suppose you're here about the manor?
dialogue.info: My family's estate lies north of the city. Strange occurrences have forced me into the town. I would pay handsomely for someone to investigate.
dialogue.farewell: Do be careful. The world is far more dangerous than it appears.

npcs.name: Colette the Flower Girl
npcs.description: A bright-eyed halfling child who sells wildflowers in the central plaza. She knows every alley and shortcut in Plassans.

dialogue.greeting: Flowers! Pretty flowers for a pretty copper! Roses, lavender, and moonpetals!
dialogue.info: I saw rats — BIG rats — coming out of the old well behind the cathedral. Like, really really big.
dialogue.farewell: Thank you! You're my favourite customer today!

npcs.name: Gaspard le Marin
npcs.description: A weathered sailor nursing a drink, waiting for his next ship. He's sailed to every port on La Côte Dorée.

dialogue.greeting: *grunt* ... Oh. Sorry. Thought you were the harbour master come to collect. What do you want?
dialogue.info: There's an island three days' sail southwest. Charts don't show it. I've seen it twice — and both times it wasn't where it was before.
dialogue.farewell: If you ever need passage, ask at the docks for the Étoile du Matin.

npcs.name: Margot Chevalier
npcs.description: An elderly woman who sits on the promenade bench watching the ships come and go, as she has done every day for thirty years.

dialogue.greeting: Sit, sit. The view is free and the memories are mine to share.
dialogue.info: I remember when a sea serpent surfaced in the harbour. The year my husband was lost. That was fifty summers ago, and still the sea does not forget.
dialogue.farewell: The tide waits for no one, dear.

npcs.name: Monsieur Leduc
npcs.description: A meticulous man in a spotless dark doublet with silver cufflinks, his fingers stained with ink from endless ledgers. He runs La Banque de Plassans with the precision of a clockwork mechanism.

dialogue.greeting: Welcome to La Banque de Plassans. How may I optimize your financial portfolio today?
dialogue.info: We offer coin exchange services — bring your coppers, leave with gold. The mathematics is simple and the service impeccable.
dialogue.farewell: A pleasure, as always. Your coins are in excellent hands — figuratively speaking.

npcs.name: Lucien the Spice Merchant
npcs.description: A dark-skinned trader from distant lands, his stall in the grand market is a riot of color and aroma.

dialogue.greeting: From the deserts of Kharim to the shores of Plassans — the finest spices in the known world!
dialogue.info: My last shipment was delayed. Storms in the strait, they say, but I've heard whispers of pirates.

npcs.name: Thérèse the Clothier
npcs.description: A prim woman with spectacles perched on her nose, surrounded by bolts of fine fabric.

dialogue.greeting: Looking for something to wear that won't fall apart in a fortnight? You've found me.
dialogue.info: The nobility are ordering mourning clothes. Something about the Beaumont family, I hear.

npcs.name: Commoner Patrice
npcs.description: A middle-aged dockworker with calloused hands and a quiet demeanor.

dialogue.greeting: Don't mind me. Just heading to the docks.
dialogue.info: Work is steady, but the harbour master has been on edge. Something about the eastern ships.

npcs.name: Commoner Adèle
npcs.description: A young woman carrying a basket of bread, hurrying home from the market.

dialogue.greeting: Excuse me! Coming through! ... Oh, hello. Sorry, I'm in a rush.
dialogue.info: Have you been to La Couronne d'Or? Aurélie's onion soup is the best thing in this city.

locationOverrides.description: The city of Plassans — jewel of La Côte Dorée. White stone buildings rise along cobbled streets, their terracotta roofs glowing amber in the coastal sun. The grand plaza buzzes with merchants and children, while the cathedral's bell tower watches over all.

pointsOfInterest.name: Cathédrale Saint-Auriel
pointsOfInterest.description: A grand cathedral with soaring stone pillars and centuries of history embedded in its walls.

pointsOfInterest.name: La Couronne d'Or
pointsOfInterest.description: The finest inn in Plassans, renowned for its onion soup and generous rooms.

pointsOfInterest.name: Le Chat Noir
pointsOfInterest.description: A rough-hewn harbour tavern where sailors swap tales and lies in equal measure.

pointsOfInterest.name: Place de la Fontaine
pointsOfInterest.description: The beating heart of Plassans — a grand plaza with a marble fountain and bustling market stalls.

pointsOfInterest.name: Le Grand Marché
pointsOfInterest.description: An open-air market brimming with spices, fabrics, and the shouts of eager merchants.

pointsOfInterest.name: Le Port de Plassans
pointsOfInterest.description: The lifeblood of the city — wooden docks stretching into the azure coastal waters.

pointsOfInterest.name: La Banque de Plassans
pointsOfInterest.description: A solid stone building with iron-barred windows and a heavy oak door. The city's premier establishment for coin exchange and financial services.

history.event: The Material Plane coalesces from the raw essence of Ylem — the primordial matter from which all things spring.
First mortal settlers arrive on the golden coast of Valterre.
Foundation of Plassans at the mouth of the Auriel River.
The Beausoleil dynasty unifies the coastal settlements into a kingdom.
Construction of the Cathédrale Saint-Auriel begins.
The Great Storm ravages the coast. Plassans rebuilds with stone walls and deeper harbours.
The kingdom of Beausoleil endures, its golden coast a beacon of civilization on the western shore.
